item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this article, it's a sad story that shows the shadows of the Herman Fuller Circus, it has my deserved +1

I would like to make an observation, I think there is an error in one of the journal dates, the date of the entry SCP-1931-DOC-B11 is 24-02-1955 while the date of the next entry SCP-1931-DOC-B12 is 09-02-1955 when it should be 09-3-1955 (the next entry is 13-3-1955)

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Alright, so with the already present link to SCP-1884, there is not a whole lot for me to really suggest. I'd argue that the mention of Herman Fuller in the opening paragraph could be linked to the GoI's Hub. The article is mentioned in the tale She Remembered Me as well, but that reference is fairly one-note and not enough to bridge back to from here. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

SCP-1931 is the head of an adult female Panthera tigris tigris (Bengal tiger), stuffed and preserved through taxidermy. A number of scars are present on SCP-1931's face, determined to have been inflicted by other animals. The wound at the base of the head has been filled with tar, presumably by the original owner. A small bronze plaque bearing the words "Property of Herman Fuller's Circus Of The Disquieting" is embedded in the tar. SCP-1931 appears to have been restored several times, primarily through the painting of its fur; however, there is no indication that the teeth have been altered in any way since the animal's death. A mechanical hinge has been installed in SCP-1931's jaw, allowing it to open and close freely.

To this:

SCP-1931 is the head of an adult female Panthera tigris tigris (Bengal tiger), stuffed and preserved through taxidermy. A number of scars are present on SCP-1931's face, determined to have been inflicted by other animals. The wound at the base of the head has been filled with tar, presumably by the original owner. A small bronze plaque bearing the words "Property of Herman Fuller's Circus Of The Disquieting" is embedded in the tar. SCP-1931 appears to have been restored several times, primarily through the painting of its fur; however, there is no indication that the teeth have been altered in any way since the animal's death. A mechanical hinge has been installed in SCP-1931's jaw, allowing it to open and close freely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the addendum-though that guy was really into his tiger-though the item itself is a little weak. Especially loved that little detail of the balloon animals-really stuck with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Paranormal circuses are wont to be compared to each other. Herman Fuller and AHS: Freakshow are absolutely no exceptions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know for some reason this always reminded me of American Horror Story Freakshow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Amelia… she can have my +1, but I don't even remotely understand why there are toes buried with her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that was depressing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loving the relationships built here, often the scientific mindset of articles overlooks the human aspects available. Here the balance was made well, take my +1, please!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you did there!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But "taxidermied" is a perfectly cromulent adjective.

Aside from which, current usage allows for nouns to be used as verbs. I googled that to be sure it was still the case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dat. Addendum. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally like it. Maybe I'm just a sucker for animals, but the story behind it still interested me, even though I have no prior knowledge of the circus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the fact that "taxidermy" is not a verb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's okay. It's certainly unique, but for me, it just seems to lack that fascination that good SCPs have. Next time, try to make it have a more exciting history, or make the object itself more anomalous. Just be careful not to overdo it, though. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome! I can't wait to see what you come up with. Someone else has told me they're planning on writing a HFCotD skip, so I added a blurb to my member page to give interested parties a general overview of how I see the Circus.

As for the GoI thing, I think of the term as more of a label than anything else. The Circus as I imagine it doesn't function like any GoI we have right now, but I wouldn't mind people calling it one as long as they don't try to impose the "rules" of other GoIs on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The anomaly itself isn't the most interesting thing, but the addenda add enough of a story to give it intrigue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I very much feel the same way. GsoI do best when it's possible to do different angles on them. This doesn't seem like it has much room for expansion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell, I love this but I don't want it to be a GoI. An article-based canon, sure (similar to Skara's Bonifay family), but not necessarily a GoI. I would, however, love to contribute to this thing when I get the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd just change him to SCP-████. The current incarnation of him wasn't ever necessarily 1234.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is better enough for an upvote, but I'm still not wild about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edrobot already fed him 682
Well you're the one who went and gave him cancer
I needed some excuse for why he's not 1234 anymore
Didn't really think that one through. Shoulda cleared it with you first, sorry. Methinks you're just playing around, but if you're really uncomfortable with it I can change it around some more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is Jester in this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you didn't have to kill Jester in the footnotes. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hence why I pointed out that I'm the only one writing these as of now. It'd feel like shameless self-promotion if I tried to make it a GoI when I'm the only one working on it. This whole circus thing just started as a personal series of interconnected articles, and although I'm open to people adding to the mythos, I'm in no hurry to get on the GoI list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also, you should wait until there's circus stuff being done by more than one writer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right now there are only 2 SCPs and 1 tale related to the Circus, and all of them were written by me. I'm not against the idea of more HFCotD SCPs and tales, but the amount of content seems way too small to be recognized as a GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I intentionally phrased the diary entry to leave it ambiguous whether or not Bernard framed Jacob. Either Bernard was innocent and he just enjoyed watching David and his brother suffer, or he talked Jacob into killing Annie and used him as a scapegoat for the other murders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a very good point. Edited accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The circus needs to become an "actual" GoI on the GoI page. There needs to be more SCPs from them. And more tales. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP very much. The effect itself is one of those "huh, so that's a thing" deals, but the additional documentation sold it. How many more are you planning to do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's this tale if you haven't read it yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the years, missed the months. Sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: G I V E M E M O R E O F T H E C I R C U S

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This still has not been taken care of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. Also, I am not fond of linking to tales without good reason — linking to other SCPs makes sense, because those are meant to be documents the Foundation has, and they would absolutely refer to another document when necessary. On the other hand, unless a tale is presented as a Foundation document, it just doesn't make sense in-universe.

I would allow it if it were presented as sort of an easter egg, like it is in SCP-355, but context clearly indicates that it's supposed to link you to a non-technical, first-hand life story of this SCP. Which is nonsensical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's not the best article on here, by any means, but it's interesting enough on its own and adds some flavor to 1884.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. I'm not sure on this. The effect itself is pretty weak, and the reliance on other items seems a bit much. The Jester connection is amusing, but it'd be a hell of a trick for this guy to frame Jacob, considering the facts as presented in that story. Also, the fangs in the picture look really fake, which contradicts what you say in the article itself. Then again, I'm no expert on tiger teeth.

The bit with him becoming okay with killing people for the circus is amusing, though I'm not sure if I like it. Document C doesn't really add much, beyond the weird thing with the toes, which is a bit too arcane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap. The source of the Palm Reader's paw… Where balloons go… The Jester, the frigging Jester! I don't know if this is too many connections, but they're all great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing that out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dates for documents B-12 and B-13 seem to be out of order; I suspect they were intended to be ten years later than indicated. Great SCP! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second Herman Fuller SCP.

Special thanks to everyone who helped on forums and on chat, including Vezaz, Mr. Wilt, and Myriad. Also, a big thank-you to Smapti for letting me allude to his wonderful Jester's Tale.

SCP-1931's theme song.

Changed image in 2019 to Tiger Head by Thomas Quine.

Discover more of my weird ramblings in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
